have h1 : DifferentiableAt ℝ GReal t := by
    -- Use the fact that G is holomorphic and the restriction to the imaginary axis is smooth
    have h₂ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
      -- Since G is holomorphic, its restriction to the imaginary axis is smooth
      -- and hence its real part is differentiable.
      have h₃ : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 G := by
        -- Prove that G is holomorphic
        have h₄ : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 G := by
          -- Use the fact that G is a product and sum of holomorphic functions
          have h₅ : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 (H₂ : ModularForm 𝕜) := by
            exact H₂_holo
          have h₆ : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 (H₄ : ModularForm 𝕜) := by
            exact H₄_holo
          -- Use the fact that the product and sum of holomorphic functions is holomorphic
          have h₇ : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 (H₂ ^ 3 : ModularForm 𝕜) := by
            exact h₅.pow 3
          have h₈ : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 (H₂ ^ 2 : ModularForm 𝕜) := by
            exact h₅.pow 2
          have h₉ : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 (H₄ ^ 2 : ModularForm 𝕜) := by
            exact h₆.pow 2
          have h₁₀ : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 (H₂ * H₄ : ModularForm 𝕜) := by
            exact h₅.mul h₆
          -- Combine all the holomorphic functions to get G
          have h₁₁ : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 (2 • (H₂ ^ 2) + 5 • (H₂ * H₄) + 5 • (H₄ ^ 2) : ModularForm 𝕜) := by
            apply MDifferentiable.add
            · apply MDifferentiable.add
              · exact h₈.const_smul (2 : ℂ)
              · exact h₁₀.const_smul (5 : ℂ)
            · exact h₉.const_smul (5 : ℂ)
          have h₁₂ : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 (H₂ ^ 3 * (2 • (H₂ ^ 2) + 5 • (H₂ * H₄) + 5 • (H₄ ^ 2)) : ModularForm 𝕜) := by
            exact h₇.mul h₁₁
          -- Convert to the correct type
          simpa [G] using h₁₂
        exact h₄
      -- Use the fact that G is holomorphic to deduce differentiability of its restriction
      have h₄ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
        -- Use the fact that the restriction of G to the imaginary axis is smooth
        -- and its real part is differentiable
        have h₅ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
          -- Apply the fact that the real part of a holomorphic function restricted to the
          -- imaginary axis is differentiable
          apply DifferentiableAt.comp (differentiableAt_id : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => t) t)
          -- Use the fact that the restriction of G to the imaginary axis is differentiable
          have h₆ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t : ℂ)) t := by
            -- Prove that the restriction of G to the imaginary axis is differentiable
            have h₇ : MDifferentiable ℝ (𝕜 := ℝ) (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t : ℂ)) := by
              -- Use the fact that G is holomorphic to deduce that its restriction is differentiable
              apply (resToImagAxis_of_MDifferentiable h₃).mdifferentiable
            -- Use the fact that the restriction is differentiable to deduce differentiability at a point
            exact h₇.differentiableAt
          -- Extract the differentiability of the real part
          have h₈ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t : ℂ).re) t := by
            apply DifferentiableAt.re
            exact h₆
          -- Convert the complex differentiability to real differentiability
          convert h₈ using 1 <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff]
          <;> norm_num
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
          <;> norm_num
          <;> linarith
        exact h₅
      exact h₄
    -- Since GReal t = (G.resToImagAxis t).re, we have the desired differentiability
    have h₃ : GReal t = (G.resToImagAxis t).re := rfl
    have h₄ : GReal = fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t).re := by
      funext t
      rfl
    rw [h₄]
    exact h₂
  exact h1

have h₁ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t : ℂ)) t := by
    -- Prove that G.resToImagAxis is differentiable at t
    apply DifferentiableAt.cpow
    · exact differentiableAt_id
    · exact differentiableAt_const _
    · simp [ht.ne']

  -- Extract the real part and check differentiability
  have h₂ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t : ℂ).re) t := by
    apply DifferentiableAt.comp t (differentiableAt_id.re)
    exact h₁
  simpa [GReal] using h₂

have h₀ : GReal = (fun (t : ℝ) => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) := rfl
  rw [h₀]
  -- Use the chain rule and the fact that the real part of a differentiable function is differentiable
  have h₁ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun (t : ℝ) => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
    -- The function t ↦ G(t) is differentiable because G is holomorphic
    have h₂ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun (t : ℝ) => G.resToImagAxis t) t := by
      -- Use the fact that the restriction of a holomorphic function to the imaginary axis is differentiable
      apply (G.holomorphic <| by
        -- Prove that G is holomorphic
        simp [G, H₂, H₄, pow_three, pow_two, mul_assoc]
        <;>
          (try infer_instance) <;>
          (try
            {
              apply MDifferentiable.holomorphic
              apply MDifferentiable.mul
              · apply MDifferentiable.pow
                · -- H₂ is holomorphic
                  have : MDifferentiable 𝓘(ℂ) 𝓘(ℂ) (fun (z : ℂ) => z) := by apply holomorphic_id.continuousAndDifferentiable
                  simpa [H₂, pow_two, pow_three] using this
              · apply MDifferentiable.add
                · apply MDifferentiable.mul
                  · -- 2 is constant
                    exact isMDifferentiable_const _
                  · apply MDifferentiable.pow
                    · -- H₂ is holomorphic
                      have : MDifferentiable 𝓘(ℂ) 𝓘(ℂ) (fun (z : ℂ) => z) := by apply holomorphic_id.continuousAndDifferentiable
                      simpa [H₂, pow_two, pow_three] using this
                · apply MDifferentiable.add
                  · apply MDifferentiable.mul
                    · -- 5 is constant
                      exact isMDifferentiable_const _
                    · -- H₂ is holomorphic
                      have : MDifferentiable 𝓘(ℂ) 𝓘(ℂ) (fun (z : ℂ) => z) := by apply holomorphic_id.continuousAndDifferentiable
                      simpa [H₂, H₄, pow_two, pow_three] using this
                    · -- H₄ is holomorphic
                      have : MDifferentiable 𝓘(ℂ) 𝓘(ℂ) (fun (z : ℂ) => z) := by apply holomorphic_id.continuousAndDifferentiable
                      simpa [H₂, H₄, pow_two, pow_three] using this
                  · apply MDifferentiable.pow
                    · -- H₄ is holomorphic
                      have : MDifferentiable 𝓘(ℂ) 𝓘(ℂ) (fun (z : ℂ) => z) := by apply holomorphic_id.continuousAndDifferentiable
                      simpa [H₂, H₄, pow_two, pow_three] using this
            }) <;>
          (try
            {
              apply holomorphic_id
            })
      ).differentiableAt.comp ht (differentiableAt_id)
    -- The real part of a differentiable function is differentiable
    have h₃ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun (z : ℂ) => z.re) (G.resToImagAxis t) := by
      simpa using (differentiableAt_id : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun (z : ℂ) => z) (G.resToImagAxis t)).comp (by
        -- The function t ↦ G(t) is differentiable at t
        apply h₂
        )
    have h₄ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun (t : ℝ) => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
      apply DifferentiableAt.comp ht h₃ h₂
    exact h₄
  exact h₁

have h₁ : DifferentiableAt ℝ GReal t := by
    -- Use the fact that G is holomorphic to deduce that GReal is differentiable at t
    have h₂ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
      -- Since G is holomorphic, G.resToImagAxis is differentiable, and hence its real part is differentiable
      have h₃ : MDifferentiable 𝓝[≠] 𝓝 (fun z : ℂ => (G z).re) := by
        -- G is holomorphic, so it is also differentiable in the real sense
        have h₄ : MDifferentiable 𝓘(ℂ) 𝓘(ℂ) G := G_holo
        -- The real part of a holomorphic function is differentiable in the real sense
        have h₅ : MDifferentiable 𝓝[≠] 𝓝 (fun z : ℂ => (G z).re) := by
          -- Use the fact that the real part of a holomorphic function is harmonic and hence differentiable
          have h₆ : MDifferentiable 𝓝[≠] 𝓝 (fun z : ℂ => (G z).re) := by
            -- This is a direct consequence of the fact that G is holomorphic
            apply h₄.differentiableAt_of_isOpen (isOpen_univ)
            <;> simp_all
            <;> aesop
          exact h₆
        exact h₅
      -- The restriction of G to the imaginary axis is differentiable, so its real part is differentiable
      have h₇ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G (↑t * Complex.I)).re) t := by
        -- Use the fact that the real part of a holomorphic function is differentiable
        have h₈ : DifferentiableAt 𝓝[≠] (fun z : ℂ => (G z).re) (↑t * Complex.I) := by
          apply h₃.differentiableAt_of_isOpen (isOpen_univ)
          <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
          <;> aesop
        -- Translate the complex differentiability to real differentiability
        have h₉ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G (↑t * Complex.I)).re) t := by
          -- Use the fact that the real part of a holomorphic function is differentiable
          have h₁₀ := h₈
          -- Convert the complex differentiability to real differentiability
          convert h₁₀.comp t (differentiableAt_id.const_smul (1 : ℝ)) using 1 <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> field_simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
          <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
          <;> norm_num
          <;> linarith
        exact h₉
      -- The function t ↦ (G(t * I)).re is differentiable at t
      have h₁₀ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (G (↑t * Complex.I)).re) t := h₇
      -- The function GReal is defined as t ↦ (G(t * I)).re
      have h₁₁ : (fun t : ℝ => (G (↑t * Complex.I)).re) = (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) := by
        funext t
        simp [resToImagAxis]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
        <;> field_simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
        <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> linarith
      -- Therefore, GReal is differentiable at t
      rw [h₁₁] at h₁₀
      exact h₁₀
    -- Use the fact that GReal is defined as the real part of G.resToImagAxis to deduce its differentiability
    have h₁₂ : GReal = (fun t : ℝ => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) := by
      funext t
      simp [GReal]
    rw [h₁₂]
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

have h1 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun (t : ℝ) => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
    -- Use the fact that the real part of a complex-valued function is differentiable if the function itself is differentiable
    have h2 : DifferentiableAt 𝕜 (fun (z : ℂ) => G z) (Complex.I * (t : ℝ)) := by
      -- Prove that G is differentiable at i*t
      have h3 : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 G := G_holo
      -- Use the fact that G is holomorphic to conclude differentiability
      have h4 : DifferentiableAt 𝕜 G (Complex.I * (t : ℝ)) := by
        apply h3.differentiableAt
      exact h4
    -- Use the fact that the real part of a differentiable complex-valued function is differentiable
    have h5 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun (t : ℝ) => (G (Complex.I * (t : ℝ))).re) t := by
      -- Use the chain rule and the differentiability of the real part
      have h6 : DifferentiableAt 𝕜 (fun (z : ℂ) => G z) (Complex.I * (t : ℝ)) := h2
      have h7 : DifferentiableAt 𝕜 (fun (z : ℂ) => (G z).re) (Complex.I * (t : ℝ)) := by
        -- The real part is differentiable because G is differentiable
        apply DifferentiableAt.re
        exact h6
      -- The real part of G at i*t is the function we are interested in
      have h8 : (fun (t : ℝ) => (G (Complex.I * (t : ℝ))).re) = (fun (t : ℝ) => (G (Complex.I * (t : ℝ))).re) := rfl
      have h9 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun (t : ℝ) => (G (Complex.I * (t : ℝ))).re) t := by
        -- Use the fact that the real part is differentiable
        apply DifferentiableAt.comp (by norm_num : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) t) h7
        <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> norm_num
        <;>
          (try
            {
              simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
              <;>
                ring_nf
              <;>
                norm_num
              <;>
                linarith
            })
        <;>
          (try
            {
              aesop
            })
      exact h9
    -- The function (G.resToImagAxis t).re is the same as (G (i*t)).re
    have h10 : (fun (t : ℝ) => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) = (fun (t : ℝ) => (G (Complex.I * (t : ℝ))).re) := by
      funext t
      simp [resToImagAxis]
      <;>
        simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
      <;>
        ring_nf
      <;>
        norm_num
      <;>
        aesop
    -- Therefore, the differentiability follows
    rw [h10]
    exact h5
  -- Since (G.resToImagAxis t).re is differentiable, GReal is differentiable
  have h2 : GReal = (fun (t : ℝ) => (G.resToImagAxis t).re) := by
    funext t
    simp [GReal]
    <;>
      simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
    <;>
      ring_nf
    <;>
      norm_num
    <;>
      aesop
  rw [h2]
  exact h1

have hG : DifferentiableAt 𝕜 (fun z : 𝕜 => G z) (t * I : 𝕜) := by
    apply DifferentiableAt.comp (k := ℂ) (G_holo.differentiableAt)
    exact differentiableAt_id.const_mul (I : ℂ)
    <;> norm_num
  -- Convert the complex differentiability to real differentiability for GReal
  have hG' : DifferentiableAt ℝ GReal t := by
    -- Use the fact that the restriction of G to the imaginary axis is complex differentiable
    -- and hence its real part is real differentiable
    have h1 : HasDerivAt GReal (Complex.re (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I))) t := by
      have h2 : HasDerivAt (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I : ℂ)) (t * I : ℂ) := by
        apply DifferentiableAt.hasDerivAt
        apply G_holo.differentiableAt
      have h3 : HasDerivAt (fun (x : ℝ) => (G (x * I) : ℂ)) (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I) * I) t := by
        have h4 : HasDerivAt (fun (x : ℝ) => (x * I : ℂ)) (I : ℂ) t := by
          simpa using ((hasDerivAt_id t).const_mul (I : ℂ))
        -- Use the chain rule to get the derivative of G at t * I
        have h5 : HasDerivAt (fun (x : ℝ) => (G (x * I) : ℂ)) (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I) * I) t := by
          -- Use the chain rule to get the derivative of G at t * I
          have h6 : HasDerivAt (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I)) (t * I : ℂ) := by
            apply h2
          have h7 : HasDerivAt (fun (x : ℝ) => (x * I : ℂ)) (I : ℂ) t := h4
          -- Use the chain rule to get the derivative of G at t * I
          have h8 : HasDerivAt (fun (x : ℝ) => (G (x * I) : ℂ)) (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I) * I) t := by
            convert HasDerivAt.comp t h6 h7 using 1
            <;> ring_nf
            <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
            <;> norm_num
            <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
            <;> norm_num
            <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
            <;> norm_num
          exact h8
        exact h5
      -- Extract the real part of the derivative
      have h9 : HasDerivAt (fun (x : ℝ) => (G (x * I) : ℂ).re) (Complex.re (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I) * I)) t := by
        have h10 : HasDerivAt (fun (x : ℝ) => (G (x * I) : ℂ)) (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I) * I) t := h3
        -- Use the fact that the real part of a complex function is the real part of its derivative
        have h11 : HasDerivAt (fun (x : ℝ) => (G (x * I) : ℂ).re) (Complex.re (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I) * I)) t := by
          convert HasDerivAt.re h10 using 1
          <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
          <;> norm_num
          <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
          <;> norm_num
        exact h11
      have h12 : (Complex.re (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I) * I) : ℝ) = Complex.re (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I) * I) := rfl
      have h13 : HasDerivAt GReal (Complex.re (deriv (fun z : ℂ => (G z : ℂ)) (t * I))) t := by
        convert h9 using 1
        <;> simp [GReal, ResToImagAxis]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
        <;> norm_num
      exact h13
    have h14 : DifferentiableAt ℝ GReal t := by
      apply HasDerivAt.differentiableAt
      exact h1
    exact h14
  exact hG'
  <;>
  simp_all [GReal, Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I, pow_two, pow_three, Complex.ofReal_mul, Complex.ofReal_add,
    Complex.ofReal_sub, Complex.ofReal_one, Complex.ofReal_zero]
  <;>
  ring_nf
  <;>
  norm_num
  <;>
  simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I, pow_two, pow_three, Complex.ofReal_mul, Complex.ofReal_add,
    Complex.ofReal_sub, Complex.ofReal_one, Complex.ofReal_zero]
  <;>
